securityPolicy.note: GitHub's standard security policy applies (private vulnerability reporting via Security tab).

signedReleases.note: GitHub signs its release artefacts; binary releases verifiable.

codeReview.note: PRs go through GitHub's internal review. Open-source repo with public history.

secretsHandling.note: PAT or fine-grained token passed via env or stdin. Never persisted in tool output. Recommends fine-grained tokens with the smallest scope possible.

credentialStorage.note: Tokens live in process env. No on-disk credential cache.

oauthHandling.note: Fine-grained PATs let you scope to specific repos and permission sets. Classic PATs work but tend to over-grant.

sandboxModel.note: Runs as a subprocess of the MCP host. No additional sandboxing inside the server.

filesystemAccess.note: Server itself doesn't touch local filesystem. Repository contents flow through API calls only.

networkEgress.note: Only api.github.com (and uploads/downloads to github.com release assets).

telemetry.note: No telemetry beyond standard GitHub API request logs (server-side, governed by GitHub's privacy policy).

dataResidency.note: Data flows through GitHub's API endpoints. Repository data residency is GitHub-managed (regional for Enterprise; US for public).

defaultPermissions.note: Depends entirely on PAT scope. A classic PAT with `repo` scope gives the agent everything across all your repos. Always use fine-grained tokens scoped to the smallest set of repos and permissions the task needs.

destructiveTools.note: MCP host (e.g. Claude Desktop) is responsible for approval prompts; the server itself does not gate destructive calls.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Tool responses include issue / PR / file content authored by third parties. Hostile content in any read result can attempt to redirect the agent. Treat all tool output as untrusted text.

supplyChainSurface.note: Go binary; dep tree small relative to JS-based MCP servers. All major deps are first-party Go libraries or GitHub-maintained packages.

depCveSurface.note: No active CVEs in the dep tree at last scan. GitHub Security Advisories monitors automatically.

rateLimitRisk.note: GitHub primary rate limit is 5,000 req/h for authenticated requests; secondary rate limits trigger on bursty patterns. Long-running agents can hit secondary limits.

maintainerType.note: Maintained by GitHub itself. Staff team. Release cadence is weekly during active development.

verdictScope: Any agentic workflow that reads or writes GitHub resources.
verdictNote: Default for code-touching agents. Adopt with fine-grained PATs scoped to the smallest set of repos and permissions the task needs; never grant `repo` (full) scope to a long-running agent.
